Name: jacobian_layer_skipping
Title: Optimizing Latency in Machine Learning Inference via Jacobian-Based Layer-Skipping Protocols
Short Hypothesis: Integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer-skipping techniques can significantly reduce inference latency while maintaining model accuracy, offering a structured approach to latency-aware machine learning designs.
Related Work: Existing literature has explored latency-aware designs primarily in MLaaS and federated learning contexts, yet few have systematically integrated PAC-Bayes principles with adaptive inference mechanisms. This proposal differentiates itself by offering a comprehensive empirical framework utilizing Jacobian dynamics, specifically targeting layer-skipping to optimize inference without compromising accuracy.
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel approach to optimize inference latency in machine learning models by integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer-skipping techniques. Our hypothesis posits that strategically skipping layers during inference, guided by the Jacobian matrix, can yield significant reductions in latency while preserving model performance. We present a rigorous experimental design that quantifies throughput and accuracy trade-offs, employing real-time latency benchmarks across various architectures. The proposed method aims to establish a new standard in latency-aware designs, providing empirical validation of performance gains while strictly adhering to memory and computational constraints. This work not only contributes to the theoretical understanding of inference dynamics but also translates into practical applications in latency-critical environments.
Experiments: Conduct experiments on standard datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) using various deep learning architectures (e.g., CNNs, Transformers).
Implement Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanisms during inference and compare the performance against baseline models without skipping.
Evaluate metrics including inference latency (in ms), accuracy (top-1 and top-5), and throughput (images per second).
Analyze the trade-offs between accuracy and latency using statistical methods to quantify the Pareto frontiers.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risk of model performance degradation if layer-skipping is not optimally configured.
Limited generalizability to architectures that do not exhibit clear layer-wise independence.
Experimental results may vary depending on the chosen datasets and architectures, necessitating careful interpretation.
